findings_details.title: /think hard is not a Claude Code slash command
findings_details.body: CLAUDE.md line 182 instructs operators: '- `/think hard` — enable extended thinking for the current turn.' The slash-command form /think hard does not exist in Claude Code. The Claude Code changelog documents the natural-language approach ('think', 'think harder', 'ultrathink' in the prompt text) but there is no /think slash command. An operator following this instruction will type /think hard expecting it to behave like /effort high and get an error or no effect. Fix: replace with the correct natural-language instruction, e.g. 'include "think hard" or "think" in your prompt — enables extended thinking for that turn.'

findings_details.title: claude --plugin <plugin-id> flag does not exist
findings_details.body: CLAUDE.md line 183 instructs: '- `claude --plugin <plugin-id>` — start a fresh session with one of the disabled plugins loaded.' The claude CLI has no --plugin flag. The help output shows --plugin-dir <path> (loads plugins from a directory) but no --plugin flag that accepts a plugin ID. An operator following this instruction will get 'unknown option --plugin' and have no way to load the disabled plugin. The correct opt-out mechanism for a project-disabled plugin is: either (a) pass --settings with a JSON override enabling the plugin for the session, e.g. claude --settings '{"enabledPlugins":{"<plugin-id>":true}}', or (b) edit .claude/settings.json to remove the false entry for that plugin. Fix: replace the incorrect CLI flag with the correct --settings syntax, or instruct the operator to temporarily edit settings.json.

findings_details.title: Plan doc still documents stale opt-out commands corrected by Round 1 fix
findings_details.body: Line 55 lists `/think hard` and `claude --plugin <name>` as the opt-out commands. Both were corrected in CLAUDE.md at commit 7b018ba: `/think hard` -> natural-language `think hard`/`ultrathink`; `claude --plugin <id>` -> `claude --settings '{"enabledPlugins":{...}}'`. The same stale forms also appear at line 206 (acceptance criteria), lines 251-253 (the verbatim content block), and line 279 (commit message template). A reader following the plan doc gets the wrong commands even though CLAUDE.md is now correct.

findings_details.title: Plan references unverifiable URL code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md
findings_details.body: Line 38 cites `https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md` as the source for Claude Code settings precedence behavior. That domain/path does not match the published Claude Code documentation location (`https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/settings`). The URL appears to be fabricated or hallucinated. The behavioral claim it supports (project-level settings.json overrides user-global) is correct, but the citation is wrong. Fix: either use the real URL or drop the `Per https://...` attribution and state the behavior as established fact.
